---
title: "Jiaju's E-Portfolio"
subtitle: "Remote sensing • Forestry • Geospatial analytics"
description: "Selected work from the MGEM program, including Garry oak detection in Victoria’s urban forest."
image: images/Jiaju_photo.jpg
about:
  template: solana
  image-width: 20em
  links: 
    - icon: envelope-at
      text: Email
      href: mailto:wujiaju@student.ubc.ca
---
##About me

Currently studying at UBC as a master student, I have the privilege to live and learn as an uninvited guest on the traditional, ancestral and unceded territories of the Musqueam people. 
I worked with a First Nation forestry venture, and I deeply appreciate this opportunity to build a real connection with "the people of the land" - the Lil'wat First Nation. 
I am working on MGEM capstone project on species detection of Garry oak trees in Victoria's urban forest using remote sensing.




## What you’ll find here

This e-portfolio showcases a small set of artifacts demonstrating how I work with data, maps, models, and diverse knowledge systems.  


## Featured highlights

- **Capstone Project**: Garry oak detection — methods, model comparison, and outputs.
- **Skills & Deliverables**: a course-by-course toolbox of geospatial and programming skills.

<!-- PHOTO PLACEHOLDER: add one banner image that represents your work:
     e.g., a field photo, a canopy map, or a LiDAR point cloud screenshot.
     Example filename: images/banner_remote_sensing.jpg -->
